vocab.ex: Ich esse einen Apfel zum Frühstück.

vocab.note: Uncountable noun — no plural like English.

vocab.ex: Die Tomate ist rot und saftig.
vocab.note: Foreign words ending in -e are often feminine.
Also called 'die Möhre' in Northern Germany!
'Kaffee und Kuchen' on Sundays is a German tradition!
Milka, Ritter Sport = famous German chocolate brands!
Umlaut plural: Vogel → Vögel. Very common!
Umlaut plural: Kuh → Kühe. German cows say 'Muh'!

vocab.ex: Mein Bruder ist älter als ich.

vocab.note: 'gern' = gladly. 'Sie tanzt gern' = she likes dancing.
Informally: 'der Opa'. Both are commonly used!
After verb: 'rot'. Before noun: 'ein rotes Auto' (ending changes)!
'blau sein' also means 'to be drunk' colloquially!
'ß' (Eszett) sounds like 's', replaces 'ss' after long vowels.
'die Wohnung' = apartment. Most Germans live in apartments!
Same form in singular and plural!
'im' = in dem. 'schlafen' is irregular: er schläft.
Irregular plural with umlaut: Buch → Bücher.
'schwer' = heavy AND difficult. Great dual meaning!
'zur' = zu der (to the). 'gehen zur Schule' = go to school.
'scheinen' = to shine AND to seem. Great double meaning!
Weather ALWAYS uses 'es': Es regnet, es schneit, es ist kalt.
'Es schneit' = It is snowing. 'schneien' = to snow.
Umlaut plural: Baum → Bäume. 'Weihnachtsbaum' = Christmas tree!
Neuter gender. 'braune' = brown (adjective ending for plural).
'Ohrwurm' = earworm (a song stuck in your head)!
'Hände schütteln' = to shake hands. Umlaut plural!
English loanword! 'tragen' = to wear. Very common!
'passen' = to fit. 'mir' = me (dative). 'Es passt mir' = It fits me.

vocab.ex: Im Winter trage ich einen Mantel.
vocab.note: 'im Winter' = in winter. Always use article with seasons!
'Autobahn' = motorway, famous for no speed limit!
'mit dem Bus' = by bus. Mit + dative = means of transport!
'pünktlich' = punctual/on time. Germans value this a lot!

vocab.ex: Das Krankenhaus ist in der Stadtmitte.

vocab.note: Female: 'die Ärztin'. Add -in for female professions!
Verb and noun share root! kochen (cook) → der Koch (chef).
'helfen' = to help + dative. 'hilft uns' = helps us.
Germany won the World Cup 4 times. National obsession!
Strong verb, uses 'sein' in perfect. 'Im Sommer' = in summer.
'zu hoch' = too high. 'zu' + adjective = too + adjective!
'Die Rechnung bitte!' = The bill please! Key restaurant phrase.
Germany is world-famous for beer! 'Prost!' = Cheers!
Speise=meal + Karte=card. Also called 'die Karte' informally.

vocab.ex: Mein Ziel ist es, Deutsch zu sprechen.
vocab.note: 'Ich habe vor, zu...' = I plan to... Key B1 structure!
'-e' ending feminine words take -en in plural.
'-ung' nouns are ALWAYS feminine. Very reliable rule!
'im Büro' = at the office. 'ins Büro' = to the office.
From 'erkälten sich' = to catch a cold. '-ung' = always feminine!

vocab.ex: Ich lade eine neue App herunter.
Die Wahl findet im Herbst statt.

vocab.note: Klima=climate + Wandel=change. Major topic in Germany!

freq.note: Most important verb. Highly irregular: ich bin, du bist, er ist, wir sind.
2nd most important. Also forms perfect tense: Ich habe gegessen.
Always capitalised in German! Unlike English 'I' — no, wait, same rule!
Goes AFTER the verb usually. 'Ich gehe nicht' = I am not going.
Feminine AND plural 'the'. Also relative pronoun 'who/which'!
Masculine 'the'. Changes case: der/den/dem/des.
Neuter 'the'. Also means 'that/which' as relative pronoun.
Does NOT change word order! 'und' is a coordinating conjunction.
Location: in + dative. Movement: in + accusative. Key distinction!
Always + dative. 'von mir' = from me. Used instead of genitive often!
Always + dative. 'mit dem Bus' = by bus. Very versatile!
Always + dative. 'zum' = zu dem. 'zur' = zu der.
Also used for masculine nouns: 'Der Hund — er ist groß.'
Lowercase 'sie' = she OR they. Capital 'Sie' = formal you!
Used for neuter nouns AND weather/time: 'Es regnet. Es ist 5 Uhr.'

freq.ex: Das Buch liegt auf dem Tisch.
freq.note: Location: auf + dative. Movement: auf + accusative.

freq.ex: Das Bild hängt an der Wand.
freq.note: For vertical surfaces and edges. 'am' = an dem.
Goes after the subject usually. 'Ich auch' = Me too!

freq.ex: Ich will, aber ich kann nicht.
freq.note: Coordinating conjunction — no word order change after 'aber'!
'noch nicht' = not yet. 'noch einmal' = once more.
'so...wie' = as...as. 'so groß wie' = as big as.

freq.ex: Erst esse ich, dann gehe ich.
freq.note: Time connector. When at start of sentence, verb comes before subject!

freq.ex: Wenn ich Zeit habe, komme ich.
freq.note: Subordinating: verb goes to END. 'wenn' = when (repeated) OR if.

freq.ex: Ich lerne, weil es wichtig ist.
freq.note: Subordinating: verb goes to END! 'weil es wichtig IST' not 'ist es'!
Subordinating conjunction. Verb goes to END: 'dass er KOMMT'.
Modal verb: conjugated verb + infinitive at END. 'Ich kann...sprechen.'
'muss' (singular), 'müssen' (plural). Strong obligation!
'will' ≠ future tense in German! It means 'want'. 'Ich will' = I want!
Permission. 'Darf ich?' = May I? Very polite question!
External obligation (told by someone else). 'Ich soll' = I am supposed to.
'mögen' = to like. 'möchten' = would like (polite form of mögen).
Uses 'sein' in perfect! 'Ich bin gegangen.' Movement verbs use sein!
'Woher' = from where. Uses 'sein' in perfect tense.
'Was machst du?' = What are you doing? Extremely common verb!
'Gibt es' = there is/are. 'Es gibt' = very important phrase!
Strong verb: sehen → siehst/sieht. 'bis bald' = see you soon!
'wissen' = know facts. 'kennen' = know people/places. Different!
'Ich denke, dass' introduces a subordinate clause. Verb to end!
'hier' = here. 'da' = there. 'dort' = over there (further away).
'jetzt' = now. 'gerade' = right now/just. Similar but slightly different!
'immer' = always. 'manchmal' = sometimes. 'nie' = never.
'sehr' = very. 'zu' = too (negative). 'Es ist zu heiß' = too hot.
'mehr' = more. 'weniger' = less. 'am meisten' = most.
'schon' = already. Also softens: 'Das wird schon klappen' = It'll be fine.
'nur' = only/just. 'Nur ein bisschen' = just a little bit.
'mal' softens commands. 'Komm mal her' = Come here (friendly, not harsh)!
'doch' contradicts a negative. 'Kommst du nicht?' 'Doch!' = Yes I am!
Adds curiosity/depth. 'Was machst du eigentlich?' = What do you actually do?
'vielleicht' = maybe. 'wahrscheinlich' = probably. 'sicher' = certainly.
'natürlich' = of course. 'selbstverständlich' = goes without saying (B2).

chunks.formal: Guten Tag, wie geht es Ihnen?
chunks.note: 'läuft's' = läuft es. 'laufen' = to run/go. Informal contraction!
'dir' = informal you (dative). 'Ihnen' = formal you (dative).
'Auf Wiedersehen' = until we see again. Very formal farewell!

chunks.de: Wie bitte? Könnten Sie das wiederholen?
chunks.formal: Entschuldigung, ich habe das nicht verstanden.
chunks.note: 'Wie bitte?' is THE phrase when you didn't hear. Works always!

chunks.en: Could you please speak more slowly?
chunks.note: 'Könnten Sie' = Could you (Konjunktiv II). Very polite!
'Meiner Meinung nach' is VERY common. Learn this phrase cold!
'Genau!' = Exactly! One word, very powerful in conversation!
Much more polite than 'Nein, das stimmt nicht!' (No, that's wrong!)
'Ich hätte gerne' is MORE polite than 'Ich möchte'. Use in restaurants!

chunks.formal: Könnten wir bitte die Rechnung haben?
chunks.note: Say this clearly to get service. Waiter won't come automatically!

chunks.formal: Entschuldigen Sie, könnten Sie mir sagen, wo... ist?
chunks.note: 'Entschuldigung!' = Excuse me! Works every time to get attention.

chunks.situation: Say you don't know a word
chunks.en: How do you say... in German?
chunks.formal: Wie lautet der deutsche Begriff für...?
chunks.note: 'Wie sagt man' = How do you say. Essential phrase for learners!

chunks.de: Kannst du das noch mal sagen?
chunks.note: 'noch mal' = once more. 'noch einmal' = same meaning, more formal.
'fallen' + dative = to find something. 'Es fällt mir leicht' = I find it easy!
'helfen' takes DATIVE. 'dir helfen' (informal). 'Ihnen helfen' (formal).

chunks.de: Vielen Dank! Das ist sehr nett von dir.
chunks.en: Many thanks! That is very kind of you.
chunks.note: 'Herzlichen Dank' = heartfelt thanks. Much warmer than just 'Danke'!
'Es tut mir leid' = It does me sorrow. Literally! Very natural apology.

chunks.de: Was hältst du davon, wenn wir...?
chunks.en: What do you think about us...?
chunks.note: 'Was hältst du davon?' = What do you think of that? Great suggestion opener!

chunks.de: Wirklich? Das kann ich nicht glauben!
chunks.note: 'Wirklich?' = Really? 'Tatsächlich' = Actually/Indeed (B1+).

chunks.situation: Say something is a good idea
chunks.formal: Das halte ich für einen ausgezeichneten Vorschlag.
chunks.note: Simple but essential. 'Das klingt gut!' = That sounds good! Also common.

verbs.ex: Ich gehe jeden Tag zur Schule.
verbs.note: Uses 'sein' in perfect tense! 'Ich bin gegangen.' Movement verbs use sein!
Vowel change: laufen → läuft (du/er). Umlaut! Uses 'sein'.
'Woher kommst du?' = Where do you come from? Uses 'sein' in perfect!
Vowel change: fahren → fährt. For any vehicle! Uses 'sein'.
'isst' (eats) vs 'ist' (is) — sounds identical! Context is key!
Strong verb: trinken → getrunken. Uses 'haben'.
Regular weak verb! kochen → kochte → gekocht. Uses 'haben'.
Strong: e→i vowel change (du sprichst, er spricht). Uses 'haben'.
Regular weak verb. 'Musik hören' = to listen to music. No 'to' needed!
Strong verb. 'einen Brief' = accusative masculine. Uses 'haben'.
Strong: e→ie change (du liest, er liest). Uses 'haben'.
Strong: a→ä umlaut (du schläfst). Uses 'haben' NOT 'sein'!
Extra -e-: arbeit+e+st (not arbeitst). Stems ending in -t add -e!
Regular weak verb. No article needed with languages: 'Ich lerne Deutsch'!
Regular. Covers sports, music AND games — very versatile!
Regular weak verb. 'auf der Party' = at the party (dative, location).
Takes DATIVE! 'mir helfen' = help me. 'dir' = you (dative). KEY rule!
'er-' prefix is inseparable. 'Kannst du...?' = Can you...? (modal)
Inseparable 'ver-'. 'Ich verstehe nicht' = I don't understand. KEY phrase!
'sich entscheiden' = reflexive. 'muss' + infinitive = must. B1 structure!

fillers.note: Untranslatable! Means 'that's just how it is'. Very common in speech!
Similar to 'halt'. 'Das ist eben so' = That's just the way it is.
Adds depth/curiosity. Makes questions sound more genuine and interested!
Contradicts negatives! 'Kommst du nicht?' 'Doch!' (Yes I am!). Unique!
Starts conclusions or transitions. 'Also...' = So... / Well then...

fillers.ex: Er ist klug. Außerdem ist er nett.
fillers.note: Adds information. 'außerdem' causes verb-subject inversion after it!
'trotzdem' = despite that. Causes inversion! Very common in B1+ speech.

fillers.ex: Obwohl es regnet, gehe ich raus.
fillers.note: Subordinating! Verb goes to END: 'obwohl es REGNET'. Expresses contrast!

fillers.ex: Es regnet. Deswegen bleibe ich zu Hause.
fillers.note: 'deswegen' = because of that. Synonym: 'deshalb', 'daher'. All cause inversion!

fillers.ex: Ich mag Obst, zum Beispiel Äpfel.
fillers.note: Abbreviated: 'z.B.' in writing. 'wie zum Beispiel' = such as.

fillers.ex: Ich kann nicht kommen, ich bin nämlich krank.
fillers.note: Explains the reason. 'Ich bin nämlich krank' = you see, I am sick!
Used to soften or clarify. 'so to speak' — adds nuance to statements.

fillers.ex: Mit anderen Worten: es klappt nicht.
fillers.note: 'klappt nicht' = doesn't work out. Great paraphrasing phrase!

fillers.ex: Das heißt, wir müssen früher anfangen.
fillers.note: Abbreviated 'd.h.' in writing. Used to clarify or conclude.

conv.title: Erste Begegnung
conv.scenario: Meeting someone for the first time
conv.tip: 'Sie' (capital S) = formal 'you'. Use with strangers! 'du' = friends and family.

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.de: Guten Tag! Ich heiße Anna.
lines.en: Good day! My name is Anna.
lines.note: 'Ich heiße' = My name is (literally 'I am called'). 'heißen' = to be called.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.de: Hallo! Ich bin Ben. Freut mich!
lines.en: Hello! I am Ben. Nice to meet you!
lines.note: 'Freut mich!' = Pleased to meet you! Literally 'It pleases me.'

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.de: Woher kommst du?
lines.en: Where do you come from?
lines.note: 'Woher' = from where. 'kommst du' = informal 'du' form.

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.de: Ich komme aus England. Und du?
lines.en: I come from England. And you?
lines.note: 'aus + country' = from + country. 'Und du?' = great conversation tip!

lines.speaker: Anna
lines.de: Ich komme aus Deutschland. Wie alt bist du?
lines.en: I come from Germany. How old are you?
lines.note: 'Wie alt bist du?' = How old are you? Very common question!

lines.speaker: Ben
lines.de: Ich bin 25 Jahre alt. Schön dich kennenzulernen!
lines.en: I am 25 years old. Great to meet you!
lines.note: 'kennenlernen' = to get to know someone. Very common phrase!

conv.title: Im Café
conv.tip: In Germany you wait for the waiter. 'Die Rechnung bitte!' to pay. Tips are not mandatory but appreciated!

lines.speaker: Kellner
lines.de: Guten Morgen! Was möchten Sie?
lines.en: Good morning! What would you like?
lines.note: 'möchten' = would like. Standard waiter question — learn this!

lines.speaker: Kunde
lines.de: Ich hätte gerne einen Kaffee, bitte.
lines.en: I would like a coffee, please.
lines.note: 'Ich hätte gerne' = I would like. MORE polite than 'Ich möchte'. Use it!

lines.speaker: Kellner
lines.de: Mit Milch und Zucker?
lines.en: With milk and sugar?
lines.note: Short questions can omit the verb in German! Very natural.

lines.speaker: Kunde
lines.de: Nur Milch, bitte. Und haben Sie Kuchen?
lines.en: Just milk please. And do you have cake?
lines.note: 'Nur' = only/just. 'Haben Sie...?' = Do you have...? (formal).

lines.speaker: Kellner
lines.de: Ja, wir haben Apfelkuchen!
lines.en: Yes, we have apple cake!
lines.note: 'Apfelkuchen' = Apfel+Kuchen. German loves compound words!

lines.speaker: Kunde
lines.de: Einen Apfelkuchen, bitte. Die Rechnung auch.
lines.en: One apple cake, please. The bill too.
lines.note: 'Die Rechnung bitte!' = The bill please! Essential restaurant phrase.

conv.title: Nach dem Weg fragen
conv.scenario: Asking for directions in the city
conv.tip: Germans are very precise with directions. They'll often give exact street names and landmarks!

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.de: Entschuldigung! Wie komme ich zum Bahnhof?
lines.en: Excuse me! How do I get to the train station?
lines.note: 'Wie komme ich zu...?' = How do I get to...? 'zum' = zu dem.

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.de: Gehen Sie geradeaus, dann links an der Ampel.
lines.en: Go straight ahead, then left at the traffic light.
lines.note: 'geradeaus' = straight ahead. 'links' = left. 'rechts' = right.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.de: Ist es weit?
lines.en: Is it far?
lines.note: 'weit' = far. 'nah' = near. Simple but very useful question!

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.de: Nein, nur fünf Minuten zu Fuß.
lines.en: No, only five minutes on foot.
lines.note: 'zu Fuß' = on foot. 'nur' = only. 'Minuten' = minutes.

lines.speaker: Tourist
lines.de: Vielen Dank für Ihre Hilfe!
lines.en: Many thanks for your help!
lines.note: 'Vielen Dank!' = Many thanks! 'für Ihre Hilfe' = for your help (accusative).

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.de: Gerne! Viel Spaß in der Stadt!
lines.en: Of course! Have fun in the city!
lines.note: 'Gerne!' = Gladly/You're welcome! Return ANY nice phrase with this!

conv.title: Teambesprechung
conv.scenario: A team meeting discussing a project
conv.tip: German meetings start on time. Direct disagreement is normal and respected. No need to be indirect!

lines.speaker: Chef
lines.de: Kommen wir zum Thema. Was ist der aktuelle Stand?
lines.en: Let's get to the topic. What is the current status?
lines.note: 'Kommen wir zum Thema' = Let's get to the point. Very German — efficient!

lines.speaker: Kollegin
lines.de: Das Projekt läuft gut, aber wir haben ein Problem.
lines.en: The project is going well, but we have a problem.
lines.note: 'läuft gut' = going well (lit. running well). 'aber' = but.

lines.speaker: Chef
lines.de: Was schlagen Sie vor?
lines.en: What do you suggest?
lines.note: 'vorschlagen' = to suggest (separable: schlage...vor). KEY business phrase!

lines.speaker: Kollegin
lines.de: Ich schlage vor, den Kunden sofort zu informieren.
lines.en: I suggest informing the customer immediately.
lines.note: 'Ich schlage vor, zu + infinitive' = I suggest doing... KEY B1 structure!

lines.speaker: Chef
lines.de: Das klingt vernünftig. Einverstanden!
lines.en: That sounds reasonable. Agreed!
lines.note: 'Einverstanden!' = Agreed! Perfect word to close a deal or discussion.
